sourceMovie.title: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes (2022) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Origin: Spirits of the Past
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes clicked for you, Origin: Spirits of the Past (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes?
faq.answer: Try Origin: Spirits of the Past, The Girl Who Leapt Through Time, Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl—each shares DNA with The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes?
faq.answer: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes (2022) is best described as Animation, Drama, Romance, Fantasy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
